summary.intent: Monitor and respond to a Codex AI review on PR #724 in the fogodev/ars-ui repository, addressing any review threads until CI and the review pass.
summary.outcome: Codex posted 3 new review threads; 2 were repeated previously-seen comments about raw `Field` tradeoffs (not acted on), and 1 actionable fixture bug was identified and fixed. A commit was made moving a valid email field outside an intentionally invalid Fieldset in both Leptos and Dioxus E2E fixtures, and pushed to the PR.

repo: The repo is fogodev/ars-ui and uses a Rust workspace with crates including ars-e2e, with both Leptos and Dioxus adapter fixture files that must be kept in sync.
CI matrix includes: formatting, snapshot checks, Clippy, Unit Tests, Coverage, Dioxus feature flags, and Release Verification — in roughly that order of completion speed.
Codex review is triggered via `@codex review` comment on the PR and signals activity with 👀 reaction; completion is detectable when the 👀 drops.
The project uses `cargo xtask ci fmt` for formatting, `cargo check -p <crate>` for per-crate checks, and `cargo xclippy` for linting.
E2E fixture files for Leptos and Dioxus live at `crates/ars-e2e/fixtures/leptos/src/categories/utility.rs` and `crates/ars-e2e/fixtures/dioxus/src/categories/utility.rs` respectively.
Commit hooks run locally and can be slow/silent — normal behavior for this repo based on prior commits.

code.finding: A valid email field was incorrectly placed inside an intentionally invalid/disabled Fieldset, causing the E2E test's valid-field assertion to fail due to inherited invalid state. Fixed by moving the valid field outside the invalid Fieldset.
Same valid-field-inside-invalid-Fieldset bug as the Leptos fixture — both adapters required the same structural fix.

workflow: Codex review state is polled via two signals: reactions endpoint (for 👀 presence) and a GraphQL query counting unresolved threads authored by 'codex'.
A 120-second polling interval was used to avoid hammering the GitHub API while waiting for long-running jobs like Coverage and Codex review.
Repeated Codex comments (the raw `Field` tradeoff threads) were recognized as non-actionable and intentionally skipped, addressing only the novel actionable finding.
The commit includes a `Co-authored-by: Codex` trailer to attribute the AI review that surfaced the bug.

friction: Local commit hook runs slowly and silently, requiring multiple `write_stdin` keepalive calls to avoid timing out — no output visible during the hook's execution.
CI GraphQL queries for check status sometimes lagged, requiring an additional wait for the result after reactions were already fetched.
Codex repeated the same raw `Field` tradeoff comment across multiple review cycles, creating noise that had to be filtered out manually each time.
open_items: Two unresolved Codex threads about raw `Field` tradeoffs were deliberately not addressed — these represent a design decision the team may need to revisit or explicitly close out.
Coverage and Release Verification CI jobs were still pending at the end of the session — their results are unknown.
PR #724 has not yet been merged; final merge approval is pending CI completion and any further review.
